qa_pairs.question: What are the Slurm options available for requesting full H100 GPUs and specific Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) instances on the Nibi cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To request one full H100-80gb GPU, use `--gpus=h100:1` or `--gpus=h100_80gb:1`. For multiple H100-80gb GPUs per node, use options like `--gpus-per-node=h100:2` or `--gpus-per-node=h100:3`, and for multiple full H100 GPUs spread anywhere, use `--gpus=h100:n` (where 'n' is the number of GPUs). For specific MIG instances, available on approximately half of the GPU nodes, you can request an H100-1g.10gb with `--gpus=h100_1g.10gb:1`, an H100-2g.20gb with `--gpus=h100_2g.20gb:1`, or an H100-3g.40gb with `--gpus=h100_3g.40gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: How can users access the Nibi cluster and launch a pre-configured JupyterLab session using the Open OnDemand (OOD) portal?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can access the Nibi cluster through a web browser by navigating to `https://ondemand.sharcnet.ca/` and signing in with MFA. Once logged into the Open OnDemand portal, to launch a pre-configured JupyterLab session, select 'Compute Node' from the top menu, then 'Nibi JupyterLab'. This will present a form to request a new session; after filling in the requirements and clicking 'Launch', users can click 'Connect to Jupyter' once the session status changes to 'Running'.
